content_en:
Note: What is commonly spread on the tongues of the people is that a woman objected to Umar’s statement and said, “You have just prohibited the people from having exorbitant dowers while Allah says in His book, ‘If you have given one of them a great amount [gintaar], do not take any of it back.’” Umar then said two or three times, “Everyone has a better understanding than Umar.” Then he went back to the minbar and said, “I prohibited you from exorbitant dower. Listen, a man should do with his wealth what he sees he should do.” This report, though, is weak and rejected. It is narrated by Mujaalid on the authority of al-Shabi from Umar. Al-Baihaqi recorded it and said, “This has a broken chain.” I say: In addition to it having a broken chain, it is weak due to Mujaalid, who is ibn Saeed, and he is not strong. Furthermore, its text is objectionable for the verse does not negate the advice that Umar gave conceming exorbitant dowers.

We must take a fresh look at this phenomenon that has spread throughout the Muslim lands, but is particularly common in the Arabian peninsula. Insisting on exorbitant dowers is actually to no one’s benefit. It has made life very difficult for the people. They, as well as both the guardians of the man and the woman, are fed up with it and becoming very unhappy with this evil custom. No intelligent person could witness the many young men who cannot get married and how they are trying to amass wealth as well as what is happening among the generation concerning women displaying their persons in public and the evil that is spreading except that he will be convinced that the dowers must be made as accessible as possible.

‘The Figh of Marriage

Since people differ with respect to how rich or poor they are, the financial situation of the prospective husband must be taken into consideration. A man must not be asked for something which is beyond what he can bear. That may force him to then seek a loan or ask others for help. In fact, it might even drive him to extend his hand to forbidden sources of wealth or to knock on the doors of questionable avenues in order to gather together the money he needs for his fiancée’s dower.

Tbn Qudaamah stated in al-Mughni,

It is not preferred to have a dower in excess of what the Prophet (peace be upon him) gave his wives. This is because if the amount of the dower is increased, the person may not be able to meet it and he may face some harm in this life and the Hereafter.!

The correct position is that it is questionable to say that exorbitant dowers are permissible unconditionally or to say that exorbitant dowers are forbidden unconditionally. The correct view is to take the different cases into consideration and conclude that if the shareeah causes are present, it is permissible to have a very large dower. However, if such a large dower leads to harm or evil, it is not permissible.

This is the choice of Shaikh al-Islam ibn Taimiya. In alIkhtiyaaraat he wrote,

If the dower that is given at the time of the marriage is a lot but the person has the means to pay it, it is not reprehensible, unless it is accompanied by an act that makes it reprehensible, such as boasting about it or something of that nature. However, if the person is not able to pay such an amount, it is disliked. In fact, it is forbidden if the person cannot amass it save by begging or other forbidden means.”

' Vol. 10, p. 101, Hajr publishing in Egypt, first edition, 1410 AH., edited by Dr. alJurki and Dr. Hilu.

Al-lkbtiyaaraat al-Fighiyah min Fataawa Shaikh al-lslaam ibn Taimiyab, p. 227, al-Sunnah al-Muhammadiyah, Cairo.

The Figh of Marriage

In Ahkaam al-Quran, Abu Bakr ibn al-Arabi stated, “The people vie in boasting about the dowers to the point that the dower reaches one million and very few can amass that amount through means which are permissible.”! Furthermore, when the husband is overburdened by the dower and other necessary spending for the marriage, he develops a hatred in his heart for his new wife and the results are the opposite of what they are supposed to be in a marriage, as was discussed earlier.

We do not reject the reality nor are we ignorant of the fact that society has different classes with respect to wealth, poverty, ability to spend and inability to spend. However, at the very least, the dower should take into consideration the social class of the husband and wife. If there is no hardship upon the man and the act is not done for boasting, competing against others or extravagance and the dower is from permissible sources, then there is no problem concerning its amount [even if it is great].

As for the poor person, he should not be asked for a dower that he cannot attain except through hardship. The guardians of the woman should not seek such from him. Remember, the dower is a gift anda present to the woman. It does not make any sense that the giver is required to provide such a present that would cause him difficulty.

At the same time, we must not get to the point that we neglect the importance of the dower and simply stress its harm and, therefore, say that it should be done away with. Certainly this cannot be done. We reject this approach from its very roots because we will never accept any going to extremes with the law of Allah concerning honoring the woman and demonstrating how precious she is. We reject this in the same way that we reject the concept that has captured the minds of many people with respect to the amount of the dower that is equally burdening our young men and women.

We also reject the justification that some young men give for refusing to marry the women of their country because they claim that the dowers are too high and they therefore marry women from other countries. We reject this because we know of many guardians who do not ask for exorbitant dowers nor do they put such as a condition for

1 Abkaam al-Quran, vol. 1, pp. 364-365.

The Figh of Marriage

marriage. On the contrary, they ask for a dower that is more of a token than anything else in order to follow the sunnah. At the same time, these same guardians give a great deal of wealth to their daughters, much more than what the husband gives to them.

When the Woman is Entitled to Her Entire Dower

The jurists agree that it is certain that the entire dower must be paid to the woman as the result of a sound marriage that has been consummated or in which the husband has died.! This is true regardless of whether the amount of the dower was explicitly stated or if it were a dower that was unstated and the same as women who are similar to the wife. None of the obligation of paying the entire dower is to be dropped unless it was already paid or if the one who has the right to it gives it up of her own accord.

In addition to the two above mentioned cases [of consummation or death], the following causes are added that also require the woman to be paid her entire dower:

According to the strongest opinion among the scholars, the woman is entitled to her entire dower as a result of a complete seclusion with her husband after a valid marriage [even if they did not consummate the marriage]. According to the Malikis, the woman is entitled to her complete dower if she spends one year in the house of her husband, even if they did not have sexual intercourse. According to the Hanbalis, she is also entitled to her complete dower if the husband is on his deathbed and divorces her only to prevent her from inheriting from him, even if such is done before consummation. Imam Ahmad specifically stated that any kind of physical pleasure, such as kissing, with the wife, even if it is less than sexual intercourse and not in private, requires that the woman receive her entire dower.

A more detailed discussion of the above aspects follows.

| Bidaayab al-Mujtabid wa Nibaayab al-Mugtasid by ibn Ibn Rushd al-Maaliki, vol. 1, pp. 22-23 (al-lstqiaamah publishing, Cairo); Qawaaneen al-Abkaam alShartyyab by ibn Juzayy, p. 226.

The Figh of Marriage

This implies any sexual intercourse or sexual contact, even if in a forbidden fashion, whether it be through the vagina or anus, as long as the man’s private part has entered the woman, and even if it be when she is menstruating, post-partum hemorrhaging, performing the pilgrimage, fasting or in seclusion in the mosque. The dower must then be paid in return for what the person has done. The man has received his right by such consummation and, therefore, the woman is entitled to her entire dower.!

This is based on Allah’s words,

WE Nyild GIA EIT y 95 1K 95 ists ohoyf Oy “But if you want to replace one wife with another and you have given

one of them a great amount [gintaar], do not take any of it back” (alNisaa 20).2 And the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) said,

TU GEES by GEKS WI 05 Se LESS ofa va

ool Me we tee ee me a ee eee oe JREad Coy eel CGB Lee oto Ob JU GS “Tf any woman gets married without the permission of her guardian, then her marriage is void; then her marriage is void; then her marriage is

void. If he consummated with her, she is entitled to her dower since her private part was taken advantage of.” If the woman is entitled to all of

5) ALFigh al-Islaami wa Adillatubu by Dr. Wahbah al-Zuhaili, vol. 7, p. 289.

[This is the verse that is mentioned in the text. Most likely, it is a mistake as this verse does not prove the point that was being made. The verse that the author was probably referring to was, “So with those of whom ou have enjoyed sexual relations, give them their dower as prescribed” (a/-Nisaa 24). This verse indicates that sexual Selations entitles the woman to her entire dower. Allah knows best —JZ]

Recorded by Abu Dawood (#2083) in the Book of Marriage, Chapter on the guardian; al-Tirmidhi (#1102) in the Book of Marriage, Chapter “There is no marriage except with a guardian”; Ibn Hibbaan ILS) and al-Haakim (vol. 2, p. 168) declared it authentic. It is a sabib hadith.
content_id:
Catatan: Yang umum tersebar di lisan masyarakat adalah bahwa seorang wanita menolak pernyataan Umar dan berkata, "Engkau baru saja melarang orang-orang dari mahar yang berlebihan sementara Allah berfirman dalam kitab-Nya, 'Jika kamu telah memberikan kepada salah seorang dari mereka harta yang banyak [gintaar], maka janganlah kamu mengambil kembali sedikitpun darinya.'" Umar kemudian berkata dua atau tiga kali, "Setiap orang memiliki pemahaman yang lebih baik daripada Umar." Kemudian ia kembali ke mimbar dan berkata, "Aku telah melarang kalian dari mahar yang berlebihan. Dengarlah, seorang laki-laki boleh melakukan dengan hartanya apa yang ia pandang perlu." Riwayat ini, bagaimanapun, adalah lemah dan tertolak. Diriwayatkan oleh Mujaalid dari al-Sya'bi dari Umar. Al-Baihaqi mencatatnya dan berkata, "Ini memiliki sanad yang terputus." Saya katakan: Selain sanadnya yang terputus, riwayat ini juga lemah karena Mujaalid, yang merupakan ibn Sa'id, dan ia tidak kuat. Lebih jauh lagi, matan-nya patut dipertanyakan karena ayat tersebut tidak menafikan nasihat yang Umar berikan mengenai mahar yang berlebihan.

Kita harus meninjau kembali fenomena yang telah menyebar di seluruh negeri-negeri Muslim, namun khususnya umum di semenanjung Arabia. Bersikeras pada mahar yang berlebihan sesungguhnya tidak menguntungkan siapapun. Hal ini telah membuat kehidupan menjadi sangat sulit bagi masyarakat. Mereka, beserta para wali dari pihak laki-laki maupun perempuan, sudah jenuh dan merasa sangat tidak senang dengan adat buruk ini. Tidak ada orang yang berakal yang menyaksikan begitu banyak pemuda yang tidak dapat menikah dan bagaimana mereka berusaha mengumpulkan harta, serta apa yang terjadi di kalangan generasi muda mengenai wanita yang menampakkan dirinya di depan umum dan berbagai keburukan yang menyebar, kecuali ia akan yakin bahwa mahar harus dibuat semurah dan semudah mungkin.

'Fiqh Pernikahan

Karena manusia berbeda-beda dalam hal kekayaan dan kemiskinan mereka, situasi keuangan calon suami harus dipertimbangkan. Seorang laki-laki tidak boleh diminta sesuatu yang melampaui kemampuannya. Hal itu mungkin memaksanya untuk mencari pinjaman atau meminta bantuan orang lain. Bahkan, hal itu mungkin mendorongnya untuk mengulurkan tangan ke sumber-sumber harta yang haram atau mengetuk pintu-pintu jalur yang meragukan demi mengumpulkan uang yang ia butuhkan untuk mahar tunangannya.

Ibn Qudaamah menyatakan dalam al-Mughni,

Tidak dianjurkan untuk memberikan mahar melebihi apa yang diberikan Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) kepada istri-istrinya. Ini karena jika jumlah mahar dinaikkan, seseorang mungkin tidak mampu memenuhinya dan ia mungkin menghadapi kesulitan di dunia ini dan di Akhirat.

Pendapat yang benar adalah bahwa dipertanyakan apakah tepat untuk mengatakan bahwa mahar yang berlebihan diperbolehkan secara mutlak atau untuk mengatakan bahwa mahar yang berlebihan dilarang secara mutlak. Pandangan yang benar adalah mempertimbangkan berbagai kasus dan menyimpulkan bahwa jika sebab-sebab yang dibenarkan syari'ah ada, maka diperbolehkan untuk memberikan mahar yang sangat besar. Namun, jika mahar yang besar tersebut menimbulkan mudarat atau keburukan, maka hal itu tidak diperbolehkan.

Inilah pilihan Syaikh al-Islam Ibn Taimiyah. Dalam al-Ikhtiyaaraat beliau menulis,

Jika mahar yang diberikan pada saat pernikahan sangat besar namun orang tersebut memiliki kemampuan untuk membayarnya, maka hal itu tidak tercela, kecuali jika disertai dengan tindakan yang membuatnya tercela, seperti membanggakan diri atau hal semisalnya. Namun, jika orang tersebut tidak mampu membayar jumlah tersebut, maka hal itu makruh. Bahkan, haram jika orang tersebut tidak dapat mengumpulkannya kecuali dengan cara meminta-minta atau cara-cara yang haram."

' Jilid 10, hlm. 101, penerbit Hajr di Mesir, edisi pertama, 1410 H., diedit oleh Dr. al-Jurki dan Dr. Hilu.

Al-Ikhtiyaaraat al-Fiqhiyyah min Fataawa Syaikh al-Islam Ibn Taimiyah, hlm. 227, al-Sunnah al-Muhammadiyah, Kairo.

Fiqh Pernikahan

Dalam Ahkaam al-Quran, Abu Bakr ibn al-'Arabi menyatakan, "Orang-orang berlomba membanggakan mahar hingga mahar mencapai satu juta dan sangat sedikit yang dapat mengumpulkan jumlah itu melalui cara-cara yang halal." Lebih jauh lagi, ketika suami terbebani oleh mahar dan pengeluaran-pengeluaran pernikahan lainnya yang diperlukan, ia menumbuhkan kebencian dalam hatinya terhadap istri barunya dan hasilnya adalah kebalikan dari apa yang seharusnya terjadi dalam sebuah pernikahan, sebagaimana telah dibahas sebelumnya.

Kita tidak menolak kenyataan dan tidak pula mengabaikan fakta bahwa masyarakat memiliki kelas-kelas yang berbeda berkenaan dengan kekayaan, kemiskinan, kemampuan berinfak, dan ketidakmampuan berinfak. Namun, setidaknya, mahar harus mempertimbangkan kelas sosial suami dan istri. Jika tidak ada kesulitan bagi laki-laki dan perbuatan itu tidak dilakukan untuk membanggakan diri, bersaing dengan orang lain, atau pemborosan, dan mahar berasal dari sumber yang halal, maka tidak ada masalah mengenai jumlahnya [meskipun besar].

Adapun orang yang miskin, ia tidak sepatutnya diminta mahar yang tidak dapat ia peroleh kecuali dengan susah payah. Para wali wanita tidak boleh menuntut hal tersebut darinya. Ingatlah, mahar adalah hadiah dan pemberian kepada wanita. Tidak masuk akal bahwa pemberi diwajibkan menyediakan hadiah semacam itu yang akan menyulitkannya.

Namun pada saat yang sama, kita tidak boleh sampai pada titik mengabaikan pentingnya mahar dan hanya menekankan mudarat-nya sehingga berkata bahwa mahar harus dihapuskan. Tentu saja hal ini tidak bisa dilakukan. Kita menolak pendekatan ini dari akar-akarnya karena kita tidak akan pernah menerima sikap berlebih-lebihan terhadap hukum Allah mengenai penghormatan kepada wanita dan menunjukkan betapa berharganya ia. Kita menolak ini sebagaimana kita menolak konsep yang telah menguasai pikiran banyak orang berkenaan dengan jumlah mahar yang sama-sama membebani para pemuda dan pemudi kita.

Kita juga menolak alasan yang diberikan sebagian pemuda untuk menolak menikahi wanita dari negeri mereka sendiri karena mereka mengklaim bahwa maharnya terlalu tinggi sehingga mereka menikahi wanita dari negeri lain. Kita menolak ini karena kita mengetahui banyak wali yang tidak meminta mahar yang berlebihan dan tidak pula menjadikannya sebagai syarat untuk

1 Ahkaam al-Quran, jilid 1, hlm. 364-365.

Fiqh Pernikahan

pernikahan. Sebaliknya, mereka meminta mahar yang lebih bersifat simbolis daripada apapun demi mengikuti sunnah. Pada saat yang sama, para wali yang sama ini memberikan banyak harta kepada putri-putri mereka, jauh lebih banyak dari apa yang diberikan suami kepada mereka.

Ketika Wanita Berhak atas Seluruh Maharnya

Para fuqaha sepakat bahwa seluruh mahar wajib dibayarkan kepada wanita sebagai akibat dari pernikahan yang sah yang telah diselesaikan dengan jima' atau di mana suami telah wafat. Ini berlaku tanpa memandang apakah jumlah mahar telah dinyatakan secara eksplisit atau apakah itu merupakan mahar yang tidak disebutkan dan sama dengan wanita-wanita yang semisal dengan istri. Tidak ada kewajiban membayar seluruh mahar yang gugur kecuali jika telah dibayar atau jika orang yang berhak menerimanya melepaskannya atas kehendak sendiri.

Selain dua kasus yang disebutkan di atas [jima' atau kematian], sebab-sebab berikut ini ditambahkan yang juga mengharuskan wanita menerima seluruh maharnya:

Menurut pendapat yang paling kuat di kalangan para ulama, wanita berhak atas seluruh maharnya sebagai akibat dari khalwat yang sempurna dengan suaminya setelah pernikahan yang sah [meskipun mereka tidak menyempurnakan pernikahan dengan jima']. Menurut ulama Malikiyah, wanita berhak atas mahar penuhnya jika ia menghabiskan satu tahun di rumah suaminya, meskipun mereka tidak melakukan jima'. Menurut ulama Hanabilah, ia juga berhak atas mahar penuhnya jika suami dalam keadaan sakaratul maut lalu menceraikannya hanya untuk mencegahnya mewarisi darinya, meskipun hal itu dilakukan sebelum jima'. Imam Ahmad secara khusus menyatakan bahwa segala jenis kenikmatan fisik, seperti ciuman, dengan istri, meskipun kurang dari jima' dan tidak dilakukan secara pribadi, mengharuskan wanita menerima seluruh maharnya.

Pembahasan yang lebih rinci mengenai aspek-aspek di atas akan disampaikan berikutnya.

| Bidaayah al-Mujtahid wa Nihaayah al-Muqtashid karya Ibn Rushd al-Maaliki, jilid 1, hlm. 22-23 (penerbit al-Istiqaamah, Kairo); Qawaaneen al-Ahkaam al-Syar'iyyah karya Ibn Juzayy, hlm. 226.

Fiqh Pernikahan

Ini mencakup segala bentuk jima' atau kontak seksual, meskipun dengan cara yang terlarang, baik melalui kemaluan maupun dubur, selama kemaluan laki-laki telah masuk ke dalam wanita, dan meskipun hal itu terjadi ketika ia sedang haid, nifas, sedang melaksanakan haji, sedang berpuasa, atau sedang beri'tikaf di masjid. Mahar kemudian wajib dibayarkan sebagai imbalan atas apa yang telah dilakukan oleh laki-laki tersebut. Laki-laki telah mendapatkan haknya melalui jima' tersebut dan oleh karenanya wanita berhak atas seluruh maharnya.

Hal ini berdasarkan firman Allah,

WE Nyild GIA EIT y 95 1K 95 ists ohoyf Oy "Dan jika kamu ingin mengganti seorang istri dengan istri yang lain, dan kamu telah memberikan kepada salah seorang di antara mereka harta yang banyak [qintaar], maka janganlah kamu mengambil kembali sedikit pun darinya" (al-Nisaa: 20).2 Dan Rasulullah (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) bersabda,

TU GEES by GEKS WI 05 Se LESS ofa va

ool Me we tee ee me a ee eee oe JREad Coy eel CGB Lee oto Ob JU GS "Siapa pun wanita yang menikah tanpa izin walinya, maka pernikahannya batal; maka pernikahannya batal; maka pernikahannya batal. Jika ia telah menggaulinya, maka wanita itu berhak atas maharnya karena kemaluannya telah dimanfaatkan." Jika wanita berhak atas seluruh

5) Al-Fiqh al-Islaami wa Adillatuhu karya Dr. Wahbah al-Zuhaili, jilid 7, hlm. 289.

[Inilah ayat yang disebutkan dalam teks. Kemungkinan besar terdapat kekeliruan karena ayat ini tidak membuktikan poin yang sedang dibahas. Ayat yang kemungkinan dimaksud oleh penulis adalah, "Maka istri-istri yang telah kamu nikmati (campuri) di antara mereka, berikanlah kepada mereka maharnya sebagai suatu kewajiban" (al-Nisaa: 24). Ayat ini menunjukkan bahwa hubungan intim memberikan hak kepada wanita atas seluruh maharnya. Wallahu a'lam — JZ]

Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Dawud (#2083) dalam Kitab Nikah, Bab tentang wali; al-Tirmidzi (#1102) dalam Kitab Nikah, Bab "Tidak ada pernikahan kecuali dengan wali"; Ibn Hibbaan ILS) dan al-Hakim (jilid 2, hlm. 168) menyatakannya sahih. Hadith ini adalah hadith sahih.
content_ar:
Note: What is commonly spread on the tongues of the people is that a woman objected to Umar’s statement and said, “You have just prohibited the people from having exorbitant dowers while الله says in His book, ‘If you have given one of them a great amount [gintaar], do not take any of it back.’” Umar then said two or three times, “Everyone has a better understanding than Umar.” Then he went back to the minbar and said, “I prohibited you from exorbitant dower. Listen, a man should do with his wealth what he sees he should do.” This report, though, is weak and rejected. It is narrated by Mujaalid on the authority of al-Shabi from Umar. Al-Baihaqi recorded it and said, “This has a broken chain.” I say: In addition to it having a broken chain, it is weak due to Mujaalid, who is ibn Saeed, and he is not strong. Furthermore, its text is objectionable for the verse does not negate the advice that Umar gave conceming exorbitant dowers.

We must take a fresh look at this phenomenon that has spread throughout the Muslim lands, but is particularly common in the Arabian peninsula. Insisting on exorbitant dowers is actually to no one’s benefit. It has made life very difficult for the people. They, as well as both the guardians of the man and the woman, are fed up with it and becoming very unhappy with this evil custom. No intelligent person could witness the many young men who cannot get married and how they are trying to amass wealth as well as what is happening among the generation concerning women displaying their persons in public and the evil that is spreading except that he will be convinced that the dowers must be made as accessible as possible.

‘The Figh of Marriage

Since people differ with respect to how rich or poor they are, the financial situation of the prospective husband must be taken into consideration. A man must not be asked for something which is beyond what he can bear. That may force him to then seek a loan or ask others for help. In fact, it might even drive him to extend his hand to forbidden sources of wealth or to knock on the doors of questionable avenues in order to gather together the money he needs for his fiancée’s dower.

Tbn Qudaamah stated in al-Mughni,

It is not preferred to have a dower in excess of what النبي ﷺ (صلى الله عليه وسلم) gave his wives. This is because if the amount of the dower is increased, the person may not be able to meet it and he may face some harm in this life and the Hereafter.!

The correct position is that it is questionable to say that exorbitant dowers are permissible unconditionally or to say that exorbitant dowers are forbidden unconditionally. The correct view is to take the different cases into consideration and conclude that if the shareeah causes are present, it is permissible to have a very large dower. However, if such a large dower leads to harm or evil, it is not permissible.

This is the choice of Shaikh al-Islam ibn Taimiya. In alIkhtiyaaraat he wrote,

If the dower that is given at the time of the marriage is a lot but the person has the means to pay it, it is not reprehensible, unless it is accompanied by an act that makes it reprehensible, such as boasting about it or something of that nature. However, if the person is not able to pay such an amount, it is disliked. In fact, it is forbidden if the person cannot amass it save by begging or other forbidden means.”

' Vol. 10, p. 101, Hajr publishing in Egypt, first edition, 1410 AH., edited by Dr. alJurki and Dr. Hilu.

Al-lkbtiyaaraat al-Fighiyah min Fataawa Shaikh al-lslaam ibn Taimiyab, p. 227, al-السنة النبوية al-Muhammadiyah, Cairo.

The Figh of Marriage

In Ahkaam al-القرآن الكريم, Abu Bakr ibn al-Arabi stated, “The people vie in boasting about the dowers to the point that the dower reaches one million and very few can amass that amount through means which are permissible.”! Furthermore, when the husband is overburdened by the dower and other necessary spending for the marriage, he develops a hatred in his heart for his new wife and the results are the opposite of what they are supposed to be in a marriage, as was discussed earlier.

We do not reject the reality nor are we ignorant of the fact that society has different classes with respect to wealth, poverty, ability to spend and inability to spend. However, at the very least, the dower should take into consideration the social class of the husband and wife. If there is no hardship upon the man and the act is not done for boasting, competing against others or extravagance and the dower is from permissible sources, then there is no problem concerning its amount [even if it is great].

As for the poor person, he should not be asked for a dower that he cannot attain except through hardship. The guardians of the woman should not seek such from him. Remember, the dower is a gift anda present to the woman. It does not make any sense that the giver is required to provide such a present that would cause him difficulty.

At the same time, we must not get to the point that we neglect the importance of the dower and simply stress its harm and, therefore, say that it should be done away with. Certainly this cannot be done. We reject this approach from its very roots because we will never accept any going to extremes with the law of الله concerning honoring the woman and demonstrating how precious she is. We reject this in the same way that we reject the concept that has captured the minds of many people with respect to the amount of the dower that is equally burdening our young men and women.

We also reject the justification that some young men give for refusing to marry the women of their country because they claim that the dowers are too high and they therefore marry women from other countries. We reject this because we know of many guardians who do not ask for exorbitant dowers nor do they put such as a condition for

1 Abkaam al-القرآن الكريم, vol. 1, pp. 364-365.

The Figh of Marriage

marriage. On the contrary, they ask for a dower that is more of a token than anything else in order to follow the السنة النبوية. At the same time, these same guardians give a great deal of wealth to their daughters, much more than what the husband gives to them.

When the Woman is Entitled to Her Entire Dower

The jurists agree that it is certain that the entire dower must be paid to the woman as the result of a sound marriage that has been consummated or in which the husband has died.! This is true regardless of whether the amount of the dower was explicitly stated or if it were a dower that was unstated and the same as women who are similar to the wife. None of the obligation of paying the entire dower is to be dropped unless it was already paid or if the one who has the right to it gives it up of her own accord.

In addition to the two above mentioned cases [of consummation or death], the following causes are added that also require the woman to be paid her entire dower:

According to the strongest opinion among the scholars, the woman is entitled to her entire dower as a result of a complete seclusion with her husband after a valid marriage [even if they did not consummate the marriage]. According to the Malikis, the woman is entitled to her complete dower if she spends one year in the house of her husband, even if they did not have sexual intercourse. According to the Hanbalis, she is also entitled to her complete dower if the husband is on his deathbed and divorces her only to prevent her from inheriting from him, even if such is done before consummation. Imam Ahmad specifically stated that any kind of physical pleasure, such as kissing, with the wife, even if it is less than sexual intercourse and not in private, requires that the woman receive her entire dower.

A more detailed discussion of the above aspects follows.

| Bidaayab al-Mujtabid wa Nibaayab al-Mugtasid by ibn Ibn Rushd al-Maaliki, vol. 1, pp. 22-23 (al-lstqiaamah publishing, Cairo); Qawaaneen al-Abkaam alShartyyab by ibn Juzayy, p. 226.

The Figh of Marriage

This implies any sexual intercourse or sexual contact, even if in a forbidden fashion, whether it be through the vagina or anus, as long as the man’s private part has entered the woman, and even if it be when she is menstruating, post-partum hemorrhaging, performing the pilgrimage, fasting or in seclusion in the mosque. The dower must then be paid in return for what the person has done. The man has received his right by such consummation and, therefore, the woman is entitled to her entire dower.!

This is based on الله’s words,

one of them a great amount [gintaar], do not take any of it back” (alNisaa 20).2 And رسول الله ﷺ (صلى الله عليه وسلم) said,

void. If he consummated with her, she is entitled to her dower since her private part was taken advantage of.” If the woman is entitled to all of

[This is the verse that is mentioned in the text. Most likely, it is a mistake as this verse does not prove the point that was being made. The verse that the author was probably referring to was, “So with those of whom ou have enjoyed sexual relations, give them their dower as prescribed” (a/-Nisaa 24). This verse indicates that sexual Selations entitles the woman to her entire dower. الله knows best —JZ]

Recorded by Abu Dawood (#2083) in the Book of Marriage, Chapter on the guardian; al-Tirmidhi (#1102) in the Book of Marriage, Chapter “There is no marriage except with a guardian”; Ibn Hibbaan ILS) and al-Haakim (vol. 2, p. 168) declared it authentic. It is a sabib الحديث.
